retrieved_docs.title: Periodontitis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: For cases of chronic periodontitis and aggressive periodontitis, both clinical, as well as radiographic examination, is mandatory. Clinical examination involves estimation of local factors, developmental discrepancies of teeth leading to increased plaque accumulation, bleeding on probing (a sign of inflammation of periodontal tissue), periodontal probing pocket depth estimation is done manually or using pressure sensitive probes, determination of furcation involvement, recession, determination of clinical attachment level. [32] Followed by clinical examination radiographic estimation of bone loss should be done using a set of intraoral periapical radiographs, bitewing radiographs or a panoramic radiograph. [32]

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Periodontal surgery should be performed after initial scaling and root planing as it will significantly reduce probing depth and attachment level gain more than periodontal surgery without initial scaling and root planing. [87]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Instruments -- Function -- Examination Kit
retrieved_docs.content: After administering local anesthesia, its efficacy can be tested using objective signs, which can put the patient at ease before initiating the surgical procedure. This includes applying firm pressure with a probe adjacent to the tooth to be extracted and down the periodontal ligament to prevent pain. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Gingivitis -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Healthy gingival tissue looks pink or pigmented in dark-skinned patients, firm, with no signs of redness or swelling, and with no bleeding after gently passing a periodontal probe along the gingival crevice. On periodontal probing, healthy gingiva shows less than 3 mm crevices, and there is no bone loss on x-rays.In many instances, gingivitis may go unnoticed by the patient as the disease may exist and progress without any symptoms. When symptomatic, the patient usually gives a history of bleeding from the gingiva while brushing, flossing, and sometimes eating particularly hard food, along with halitosis that does not resolve even after performing oral hygiene. Physical examination of the oral cavity reveals the presence of an inflamed and tender gingiva that usually bleeds on gentle probing. The gingival margins that show a knife-edge appearance and the gingival tissue with a stippled aspect found in healthy gingiva are replaced by a more rounded and shiny aspect. Significant plaque and calculus deposits are usually seen. In chronic gingivitis, the size of the gingival tissue may be increased towards the incisal due to edema or hyperplasia, resulting in probing depths more than 3 mm; however, no attachment loss has occurred. These are known as false pockets.

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontal Abscess -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: If the cause of the abscess is an embedded foreign object, this must be removed through debridement, and drainage through the gingival sulcus should be performed either with a periodontal probe or by smooth scaling. Finally, a warm saline rinse is indicated. Follow up after one to two days. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontal Abscess -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Partial root fracture: fractures are detectable through visual inspection, increased mobility, and tenderness. Taking radiographs from different angles can help to detect fractures. Similarly, endodontic perforations or perforations resulting from posts are also detectable through intraoral radiographs of alternative angulations. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The success of non-surgical periodontal therapy is evaluated 6 to 8 weeks after scaling and root planing. [5] This re-evaluation aims to identify any persistent signs of periodontitis, including ongoing gingival inflammation, deep probing depths, continued attachment loss, gingival recession, or worsening tooth mobility. Based on the concept of critical probing depth, probing depths of 5 mm or less at the re-evaluation stage should be treated nonoperatively. In contrast, sites with probing depths of 6 mm or more will likely benefit from periodontal surgery. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Pockets with depths of 7 mm show the most significant pocket depth reduction with periodontal surgery but also greater gingival recession than scaling and root planing. [30] Pocket depth reduction is similar for gingival flap and osseous surgery for pockets greater than 7 mm, but osseous surgery produces more gingival recession. [88] Regenerative periodontal therapy for infrabony pockets generally results in attachment gain and radiographic bone fill, with deeper and more narrowed angled bone defects favoring regeneration. Regeneration is typically similar to any regenerative material. [89]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Roots -- Equipment
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of dental roots requires many of the same surgical instruments in conventional exodontia. The typical surgical setup usually includes a combination of the following: Elevators Luxators Forceps Root tip picks Retractors Curets Dental explorers Dental mirrors Scissors Scalpels Hemostats Periodontal Probes Rongeurs Bone files Local anesthetic needles and syringes Irrigation needles and syringes Local anesthetic Sterile gauze Sutures Surgical handpiece Surgical bur Sterile saline Sterile water Bite block

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontal Disease -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Periodontitis can affect individuals of any age, but is more commonly seen in middle-aged and older adults. CAL is used to determine disease severity—mild (1–2 mm), moderate (3–4 mm), or severe (>5 mm). [32] Routine dental screenings are essential for early detection and timely intervention. Reviewing the patient’s medical history can help identify underlying risk factors. Probing depths greater than 3 mm indicate periodontal disease, while depths exceeding 5 mm often necessitate more intensive treatment, as these areas are more difficult to manage mechanically. Dental x-rays are also used to evaluate the extent of alveolar bone loss. [1] Please see StatPearls' companion resource, " Periodontitis ," for more information.

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The periodontal examination includes measuring the width and thickness of the attached gingiva, the clinical and attachment level, the crestal bone level concerning the CEJ, and probing depths. [1] A clinical short tooth can be due to gingivitis, gingival hyperplasia, altered passive eruption, or tooth wear, and examining the periodontal tissues helps identify the etiology. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontal Abscess -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Periapical radiographs are critical in evaluating the periodontal hard tissues. Widening of the periodontal ligaments and horizontal or vertical bone loss is often expected.

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck, Teeth -- Surgical Considerations
retrieved_docs.content: When making a treatment plan for a questionable tooth, many factors come into play to decide whether the tooth should be treated and maintained, or extracted and replaced by an implant. From a periodontal standpoint, the prognosis of a tooth is determined by its amount of attachment loss and probing pocket depth. The progressive loss of periodontal attachment and increase in probe pocket depth are predictive of additional disease activity increases the likelihood of needing to be extracted. If the bone loss is too great with the periodontal loss, an implant would only be a possible replacement with successful bone volume augmentation. From a tooth structural standpoint, the prognosis of a tooth is determined by its amount of coronal structure remaining. A prosthetic crown can be placed on the tooth if there is sufficient structure remaining to retain the crown. Crown lengthening and extrusion can increase the amount of tooth structure for sufficient retention of a dental crown when appropriate. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontal Abscess -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Periodontal abscesses have a higher incidence among patients with preexisting periodontal pockets. [1] In a longitudinal study in Nebraska, researchers followed 51 patients with periodontitis over a 7-year time frame, with 27 of these eventually presenting with a periodontal abscess. Twenty-three of the abscesses developed in teeth treated with coronal scaling only, three in teeth that received root planing, and one where the tooth needed treatment with flap surgery. Sixteen out of the twenty-seven abscesses had initial probing depths greater than 6 mm. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Periodontal Disease -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: After nonsurgical periodontal treatment, probing depths reduce as healing occurs with the formation of a long junctional epithelium. This healing process involves tissue development that mirrors the histology of the original junctional epithelium. During periodontitis, connective tissue is destroyed, causing the junctional epithelium to elongate until it reaches unaffected connective tissue. This marks the cessation of migration and the formation of the long junctional epithelium.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2813
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrasonography of head and neck is classified as a form of diagnostic ultrasound of the head and neck.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 72
retrieved_docs.content: The category “diseases of mouth; excluding dental” is classified as a subcategory of “اِضطِراب الغدة اللُّعابِيَّة” (salivary gland disorder), indicating that non‑dental oral diseases are considered part of the broader spectrum of salivary gland pathology. This relationship highlights the importance of evaluating salivary gland function when assessing oral conditions that are not of dental origin, thereby guiding more comprehensive diagnosis and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2432
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester, is mapped to short cervical length in pregnancy, indicating that the condition of cervical shortening at any stage of gestation is understood as a short cervical length—a key obstetric measurement used to evaluate the risk of preterm birth and guide patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1491
retrieved_docs.content: Autoradiografie, diagnostické techniky radioizotopové a diagnostické techniky otologické jsou všechny metodiky, u nichž lze uplatnit přístrojové vybavení jako povolený kvalifikátor. Stejně tak náhražky mléka, jiné respirační terapie, peritoneální dialýza a obecně i přístrojové vybavení samotné mohou být kvalifikovány pomocí přístrojového vybavení, což zahrnuje i obecnou anestezii (التخدير العام). Přístrojové vybavení může být dále specifikováno scintilací – počítáním a emisní počítačovou tomografií, čímž se rozšiřuje jeho využití v nukleární medicíně. Non‑imaging nuclear medicine probe or assay představuje centrální prvek, který je spojen s autoradiografií a má širší vztah k diagnostickým technikám radioizotopovým. Tento typ sondy nebo assay je součástí nukleární medicíny a je také propojen s scintilací – počítáním a emisní počítačovou tomografií. K jeho funkci jsou využívány radionuklidy jako látky, které jsou v tomto kontextu používány. Celkově tedy přístrojové vybavení slouží jako klíčový kvalifikátor pro širokou škálu diagnostických a terapeutických postupů – od autoradiografie a radioizotopových diagnostických technik po otologické vyšetření, peritoneální dialýzu, respirační terapii a obecnou anestezii – a je úzce propojeno s neobrazovými sondami a assayemi nukleární medicíny, které využívají radionuklidy a jsou podporovány metodami jako scintilace – počítání a emisní počítačová tomografie.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1954
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic procedures on nose; mouth and pharynx are categorized as a sub‑type of عمليات الأنف, indicating that they fall within the broader group of nose‑related surgical interventions. These diagnostic procedures are closely linked to منظار داخلي, an internal endoscopic technique that is commonly employed to visualize and assess the nasal cavity, oral cavity, and pharyngeal structures. Understanding that diagnostic procedures on nose; mouth and pharynx belong to عمليات الأنف and utilize منظار داخلي helps clinicians select appropriate endoscopic tools for accurate evaluation and management of upper airway conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2808
retrieved_docs.content: Aspiration curettage following delivery or abortion is classified as a form of dilatation and curettage (D&C) performed as an aspiration procedure after delivery or abortion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 1 -- Community 170
retrieved_docs.content: adverse‑event reporting and clinical management. Recognizing sodium monofluorophosphate as a fluoride source guides oral‑health preventive strategies, while awareness of the SMQ classification aids clinicians in systematic identification and treatment of functional GI disturbances, underscoring the importance of these entities for patient care and product design.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2721
retrieved_docs.content: Other conditions or status of the mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium are associated with a uterine size for dates discrepancy in women who have a child, indicating that maternal health issues that affect gestation can manifest as mismatches between expected uterine size based on gestational age and the actual uterine measurements in mothers with existing offspring.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 4 -- Community 2878
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnostic procedures on the nose, mouth, and pharynx are classified as a subcategory of عمليات الأنف, indicating that these examinations are considered part of the broader group of nasal operations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 570
retrieved_docs.content: preterm birth, chorioamnionitis), maternal factors (cervical pathology, ectopy), delivery modes (cesarean, forceps, vacuum‑assisted, extraction), fetal findings (missed abortion, low birth weight), and pregnancy classifications (selective, optional termination), providing a comprehensive framework for perinatal screening, clinical decision‑making, and management of maternal‑fetal health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1929
retrieved_docs.content: Nasal endoscopy with maxillary antrostomy is classified as a diagnostic procedure involving the nose, mouth, and pharynx, providing clinicians with direct endoscopic visualization and access to the maxillary sinus for assessment of sinonasal pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1989
retrieved_docs.content: Corticosteroids for local oral treatment and other agents for local oral treatment in ATC are both classified as stomatological preparations, indicating that each is a type of medication formulated for use within the oral cavity to manage dental or mucosal conditions.
